track: 3 — recall/discovery (Shape-B: precision / silence on real MULTI-COMPONENT code)
shape: B (precision-silence — 0 defects; the value is the named suppressions across the CROSS-MODULE families kemal could not exercise)
engine_proxy: deterministic-detector-sweep (4 read-only Explore agents, one per family, applying the deterministic IF-8/IF-6/IF-10/IF-7c predicates to the real source — NOT a full Detect→Emit pipeline; no cost ledger recorded, cost stays ). Build chain: wf wlwsejivw (4 agents, ~204k tokens, 286 tool-uses).

repo.name: pyccel/pyccel
repo.stack: Python (AST compiler / transpiler to C & Fortran)
repo.size: 157 files, ~59k code LOC (scc), ~11 components (ast, codegen, commands, compilers, complexity, errors, extensions, naming, parser, stdlib, utilities)

result_headline: 0 fires across all 4 deterministic detectors on a real 59k-LOC MULTI-COMPONENT Python codebase, with ~27 near-candidates suppressed by NAMED structural guards. Exercises the cross-module families (IF-8 module graph / IF-6 cross-module / IF-10 cross-module) that the single-component kemal sweep could not — and the suite stays correctly silent on a mature codebase (comprehension, not keyword-matching).

per_family.finding: Clean acyclic module-level dependency graph across all 11 components — verified by actual module-load testing, zero `TYPE_CHECKING` blocks, no deferred/lazy module-boundary cycles. No strongly-connected component of size ≥2. The first real multi-component test of IF-8: it correctly fires nothing on a genuinely acyclic graph (not vacuously — the agent built and load-tested the full graph).

The `keywords` sets in the 4 language name-clash checkers (C / C++ / Fortran / Python at `naming/*nameclashchecker.py`) have genuinely DIFFERENT membership (Fortran adds `allocatable`/`forall`/`pure`; Python is empty) — a naive 'these keyword lists differ → divergence' detector would fire. The guarded detector correctly SUPPRESSES all 5: they are **class attributes in a polymorphic class hierarchy** (base `languagenameclashchecker.keywords = None`, each language subclass overrides), NOT module-level same-name conflicting constants. Per-language intentional overrides, not a divergent-named-set defect. (Comprehension: a class-hierarchy override is by-design variation, not an accidental conflict.)

Three `DEBUG = False` module-level literals (`parser/syntax/{headers,openacc,openmp}.py`) each gate a local `if DEBUG:` — but NONE is imported cross-module (verified: only `parse`/exception types are imported from those modules). The cross-module IF-10 §29 slice requires the const to cross a component edge (clause C3), so all 3 are correctly suppressed as local-only. (Note: the in-file IF-10 §25 slice would fire LOW on these as 'parked debug code — statically unreachable, not deletable' — the documented `parked feature vs dead code` framing; the §29 cross-module sweep correctly leaves them to §25.)

~19 swallowing/except-pass handlers examined, EVERY ONE suppressed by a named reason across four categories: (a) **deliberate fallback** — `variable.py:725/779` retain pre-try values on a failed int/step conversion; `library_config.py:526` returns `None` with a downstream guard; `parser/base.py:103` tries shorter module paths in a loop; (b) **`errors.report(severity='fatal')` re-raise** — `ccode.py:1676/1691`, `cwrapper.py:1338`, `extend_tree.py:92` raise a PyccelError (a swallow-then-re-raise, not a swallow); (c) **expected-condition with a documented comment** — `semantic.py:5581/6980` KeyError = a normal not-yet-created / not-in-set state with continuing control flow; (d) **`sys.exit()` termination / entry-point / module-level optional-import fallback** — `pyccel_config.py`, `pyccel_test.py`, `console.py`, `errors.py` (termcolor fallback). A naive `except: pass` linter would over-fire on the 8 bare `except: pass` sites alone.

naive_vs_guarded_illustrative.note: Illustrative across all 4 families (detector-pattern near-misses, not a single rigorously enumerated class). A naive structural linter would over-fire on ~27 sites (5 different-membership keyword sets + 3 DEBUG const-branches + ~19 swallowing handlers); the guarded suite raises 0, each suppression grounded to a file:line + a named reason.

honest_assessment: pyccel is a mature, well-structured multi-component compiler, so 0 raised issues is the correct precision outcome — a Shape-B scope-honesty datapoint, NOT a recall measurement (there is nothing to recall here). The value is that it exercises the CROSS-MODULE deterministic families on real 59k-LOC Python — the IF-8 import graph (built + load-tested, genuinely acyclic), IF-6 cross-module set-diff (correctly distinguishing polymorphic class-attribute overrides from a divergent named set), IF-10 cross-module const-fold (correctly distinguishing local from imported flags), and IF-7c cross-boundary swallow (correctly reading errors.report(fatal) as a re-raise and expected-condition KeyErrors as normal flow) — the families the single-component kemal sweep structurally could not reach. Across the two sweeps (kemal Crystal + pyccel Python), DeepInit's deterministic detector suite ran over ~61k LOC of real off-/non-fixture code and raised ZERO issues, with ~31 near-candidates each suppressed by a NAMED structural guard and 0 false defects. No cost ledger is recorded (a direct detector sweep is not a representative full Detect→Emit run;  cost still needs a real full-pipeline run).
